User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user (either student or professor) in the FitAssist application.

PhysicalAssessment.title: PhysicalAssessment
PhysicalAssessment.description: Represents a physical assessment record for a student.

id.description: Unique identifier for the physical assessment.

date.description: The date of the physical assessment.

CircumferenceMeasurement.title: CircumferenceMeasurement
CircumferenceMeasurement.description: Represents a circumference measurement taken during a physical assessment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the circumference measurement.

AdiposityMeasurement.title: AdiposityMeasurement
AdiposityMeasurement.description: Represents an adiposity measurement (skinfold) taken during a physical assessment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the adiposity measurement.

BioimpedanceAnalysis.title: BioimpedanceAnalysis
BioimpedanceAnalysis.description: Represents bioimpedance analysis data for a student.

id.description: Unique identifier for the bioimpedance analysis.

StrengthAssessment.title: StrengthAssessment
StrengthAssessment.description: Represents a strength assessment for a student.

id.description: Unique identifier for the strength assessment.

bryzickRepetitionsMax.description: Result of the Bryzick Repetitions Max test.

VO2MaxAssessment.title: VO2MaxAssessment
VO2MaxAssessment.description: Represents a VO2Max assessment for a student.

id.description: Unique identifier for the VO2Max assessment.

TrainingProgram.title: TrainingProgram
TrainingProgram.description: Represents a training program designed for a student.

id.description: Unique identifier for the training program.

name.description: The name of the training program.

description.description: A description of the training program.

WorkoutSession.title: WorkoutSession
WorkoutSession.description: Represents a workout session within a training program.

id.description: Unique identifier for the workout session.

date.description: The date of the workout session.

pleasureScale.description: Pleasure scale rating for the workout session.

pseSession.description: PSE (Perceived exertion) for the workout session.

intentionToRepeat.description: Intention to repeat the workout session.

recoveryPerception.description: Perception of recovery after the workout session.

Exercise.title: Exercise
Exercise.description: Represents a specific exercise within a workout session.

sets.description: Number of sets for the exercise.

reps.description: Number of repetitions for the exercise.

weight.description: Weight used for the exercise in kilograms.

rpe.description: Rate of Perceived Exertion (RPE) for the exercise.

definition.description: Stores user profile data.  Accessed by the user themselves. The {userId} is the Firebase Auth UID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the user.

definition.description: Indicates that a professor has access to a student's data. Existence of the document grants read access to the student's data under /users/{studentId}. Professors can only see the data of students they are associated with. Data in this collection are NOT used to authorize access to the student document. This achieves authorization independence.

params.name: professorId
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the professor.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the student that the professor has access to.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore structure is designed to support the FitAssist application, providing secure and scalable data storage for user profiles, fitness assessments, and training programs. It incorporates key principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling to ensure robust security rules and simplified data management.

**Authorization Independence:** Authorization independence is achieved by avoiding `get()` calls in security rules. User data and related assessments are stored under their respective user IDs, eliminating the need to fetch parent document data for authorization.  Professor access to student data is managed via the `/professors/{professorId}/students/{studentId}` collection, which grants read access to the student's data located under `/users/{studentId}`.  Critically, no security rules will rely on reading data from `/professors/{professorId}/students/{studentId}` to enforce access to student data; its existence is sufficient for read access. The professor 'membership' is checked via document existence, avoiding content-based rules. This ensures that read operations remain atomic and that security rules can be evaluated efficiently.

**Structural Segregation:** The structure separates user data (students and professors) into distinct collections.  Student-specific data, like physical assessments and training programs, are further segregated under the `/users/{userId}` path. Professor access to student data is managed through a separate `/professors/{professorId}/students/{studentId}` collection. This segregation simplifies security rules by ensuring that each collection has a clear and consistent security posture.

**Access Modeling:**
*   **User Data:**  User profiles are stored in the `/users/{userId}` collection, using path-based ownership for private data. Each user's ID serves as the document ID, providing simple and secure access control.
*   **Fitness Assessments:** Physical assessments and related data (circumference, adiposity, bioimpedance, strength, VO2max) are stored as subcollections under `/users/{userId}/physicalAssessments/{assessmentId}`. This hierarchical structure maintains ownership and allows for easy querying of assessments for a specific user.
*   **Training Programs:**  Training programs and workout sessions are stored as subcollections under `/users/{userId}/trainingPrograms/{programId}`. This allows secure, user-scoped access and management of training content.
*   **Professor Access:** Professors can access student data via the `/professors/{professorId}/students/{studentId}` collection. The presence of a document in this collection grants read access to student data. The security rules do not need to read the contents of this document.  This leverages "existence over content".

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** This structure addresses QAPs by ensuring that `list` operations can be performed securely. For example, listing physical assessments for a user is done directly on the `/users/{userId}/physicalAssessments` collection, which only contains assessments belonging to that user.  Professor read access to student documents is controlled by requiring the existence of a document in the `/professors/{professorId}/students/{studentId}` collection, which allows efficient listing of students for a particular professor.
